mnemonics.title: OSI Layer Order
mnemonics.content: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away - Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application

mnemonics.title: Data Link Functions
mnemonics.content: FEFE - Framing, Error control, Flow control, Access control

mnemonics.title: Error Detection Methods
mnemonics.content: PCP - Parity, CRC (Polynomial), Checksum - the three musketeers of error detection

mnemonics.title: Error Types
mnemonics.content: SABE - Single bit errors Are Bad, Burst errors are Worse (or SA Berries Eat - Single bit vs Burst)

mnemonics.title: Sub-layers
mnemonics.content: MAC lower, LLC upper - MAC addresses Machines, LLC handles Links

acronyms.usage: The 7-layer reference model for network communication, with Data Link Layer as Layer 2
Upper sublayer of Data Link Layer managing frame synchronization, flow control, and error handling
Lower sublayer of Data Link Layer handling node addressing (MAC addresses) and medium access
Powerful polynomial-based error detection method using binary division to generate check bits

acronyms.expansion: Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection
acronyms.usage: MAC protocol for Ethernet where nodes listen before transmitting and detect collisions
Error control mechanism where receiver requests retransmission when errors are detected

visualTips.visualization: Imagine counting eyes on dice - even parity means total 1s including parity bit must be even (like rolling 2, 4, 6). The parity bit is the extra eye needed to make the count right.

Picture long division with binary numbers where the remainder becomes the Frame Check Sequence (FCS). The divisor is a chosen polynomial, and the receiver re-divides to verify - if remainder is zero, no error!
Single-bit error is like a single raindrop hitting one pixel on a screen. Burst error is like a waterfall splashing across multiple consecutive pixels - much harder to fix!

Think of frames as letters in envelopes - the header is the address (source/destination MAC), the data is the letter inside, and the trailer is the seal (FCS) that verifies the letter wasn't tampered with.

analogies.analogy: Like a postal system: you put your letter (data) inside an envelope (frame) with sender and receiver addresses (MAC addresses), add a seal (FCS/trailer), and mail it. The post office handles the sorting and delivery between adjacent offices (nodes).

Like a checksum on a package delivery - you count items in a box before shipping, write the total on the box, and the receiver counts again. If numbers don't match, something went wrong during shipping.

Like a water faucet filling a bucket with a hole. If you open the faucet full blast, the bucket overflows (buffer overflow). Flow control is like adjusting the faucet speed to match how fast water leaks out - just enough to fill without spilling.

Like people talking in a meeting - before speaking, you listen (carrier sense). If two people start talking at once, they both stop (collision detection), wait random times, then try again. This prevents talking over each other.
Like static on a radio during a thunderstorm - the lightning creates a burst of interference that corrupts many consecutive notes of music, rather than a single random crackle.
